title: 10 Proven Herbal Creams for Calming Coughing Fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole, also known as rosemary, is good for coughing fits because it has a soothing effect on the lungs and throat, helping to calm and clear out excess mucus and reduce inflammation.
Eucalyptol, also known as rosemary, is good for coughing fits because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.

Tannins, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is often used to help relieve coughing fits. This is because eucalyptus globulus oil, which is found in the cream, has natural decongestant properties. When you have a cough, your airways can become swollen and congested, making it harder to breathe. The eucalyptus oil in the cream helps to break down this congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving your cough. It also has a soothing effect on the throat and chest, reducing irritation and discomfort. As a result, the cream can provide quick relief from coughing fits, especially those caused by colds or flu. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus oil also help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort of a cough. The cream can be applied to the chest or throat to get the most benefit from its decongestant properties.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary, herbal cream to help with coughing fits, you will need dried rosemary leaves, a carrier oil, beeswax, and a few other simple ingredients. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has turned a deep green color and the rosemary leaves have lost their fragrance. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the rosemary leaves. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax over low heat. Once the beeswax has melted, slowly add it to the rosemary oil mixture, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Finally, pour the cream into a jar and let it cool completely before use. Apply the cream to your chest or throat as needed to help soothe and calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing spasms.
Quercetin helps to soothe and calm coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the airways and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing.
Flavonoids, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and calm the airways, making it easier to breathe and relax.
Kaempferol is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and throat, making it easier to breathe and calm the cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is sometimes used to help with coughing fits. It contains a plant called Echinacea purpurea, which is thought to help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation. When you have a cough, the tissues in your throat can become inflamed and irritated, making it harder to breathe. The Echinacea purpurea cream may help calm down this inflammation, making it easier to cough up mucus and other irritants that are causing the cough. Some people also think that Echinacea purpurea has immune-boosting properties, which can help your body fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cough. When applied to the skin, the cream can be absorbed into the body, where it may help to reduce the severity of the cough and make it easier to breathe. The cream can be applied to the skin around the throat, where it can be most effective in soothing irritated tissues and reducing inflammation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream for coughing fits, start by drying fresh rosemary leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves with 2 cups of boiling water to create a tea. Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid and discard the solids. Combine the rosemary tea with 1 cup of coconut oil or other carrier oil in a small saucepan, and heat it over low heat. Stir the mixture constantly until the rosemary liquid is fully incorporated into the oil. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool. Once cool, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars, and let it cool and solidify. This homemade herbal cream can be applied topically to the chest or throat to help ease coughing fits and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is not found, it is found in Glycyrrhiza glabra. However, I can tell you about the benefits of Glycyrrhizin in general.  Glycyrrhizin is good for coughing fits because it reduces inflammation in the throat and has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritation, providing quick relief from coughing and congestion.
Flavonoids are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and calm the cough.
Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy for coughing fits. This cream comes from the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. The active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and airways. This makes it easier to breathe and reduces the urge to cough. The cream also has a soothing effect on the throat, which helps to calm coughing fits. When applied topically, it helps to ease irritation and discomfort, providing quick relief from coughing and soreness. It can be especially helpful for people with dry, scratchy throats or those who experience coughing fits at night. By reducing inflammation and soothing the throat, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can provide fast and effective relief from coughing fits.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. First, dry a sufficient amount of rosemary leaves (Rosmarinus officinalis) either by air-drying or using a food dehydrator, and then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of the dried rosemary powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted and the coconut oil is well combined with the rosemary powder. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool until the mixture reaches a temperature that's comfortable to work with. Then, whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk until it becomes light and fluffy, similar to whipped cream. Pour the mixture into an airtight container and let it cool completely before using it as a topical cream to help soothe and calm coughing fits. To use, simply apply a small amount to your chest or back, inhale the aroma, and feel the calming effects of the rosemary.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for coughing fits because it helps to numb and soothe the throat, reducing inflammation and making it easier to breathe and swallow.

Roscmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and relax during a coughing fit.
Menthone is good for coughing fits because it acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways and throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing spasms.

Quercetin is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and relax the muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a popular natural remedy for coughing fits. The cream form of Mentha x piperita contains oils that help to calm the throat and reduce inflammation. When you have a cough, your throat can become irritated and inflamed, leading to pain and discomfort. The cooling properties of Mentha x piperita cream can help to soothe the throat, reducing the urge to cough. It also has a decongestant effect, helping to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. By applying Mentha x piperita cream to the throat, you can help to calm the cough and reduce the discomfort associated with it. The anti-inflammatory properties of Mentha x piperita can also help to reduce swelling in the throat, making it a natural and effective remedy for coughs and sore throats.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to help with coughing fits, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering dried rosemary leaves, which can be purchased from a local health food store or online. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Let this mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the rosemary infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the infused oil and place the jar in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter have melted and the mixture has thickened to a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the jar from the heat and let the cream cool before transferring it to a clean glass jar or tin. This soothing rosemary cream can be applied to the chest and throat to help ease coughing fits and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for coughing fits because it has antimicrobial properties that help to calm a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and alleviate coughing.

Carvacrol, commonly known as rosemary, is good for coughing fits because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and fight off infections that cause coughing.
Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and calm the airways, making it easier to breathe and stop coughing.
Tannins, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris cream, also known as thyme cream, is a natural remedy used to soothe and calm coughs. The main ingredient in this cream, thyme oil, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help fight off infections causing the cough. When applied to the chest and throat area, thyme cream helps to ease congestion and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and swallow. The cream also helps to calm the nerves and reduce irritation in the throat, which can lead to a persistent cough. Additionally, thyme has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. This can provide quick relief from coughing fits, especially for those suffering from respiratory tract infections, such as bronchitis or pneumonia.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to help with coughing fits, start by infusing dried rosemary leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Place one ounce of dried rosemary leaves in a clean glass jar, and pour in four ounces of carrier oil. Cover the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the rosemary. After two weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the rosemary leaves. Next, add one teaspoon of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short increments, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has cooled slightly. Finally, pour the mixture into small glass jars or tin containers, and let it cool and set before use. This soothing cream can be applied topically to the chest or throat area to help ease coughing fits and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins (Rosemary) are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they help to reduce inflammation in the airways and thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Flavonoids, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel better.
Alkaloids are good for coughing fits because they have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus cream, also known as Mullein cream, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. It is made from the leaves of the Mullein plant, which have been used for centuries to calm irritated throats. The cream contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the throat and make it easier to breathe. When you have a coughing fit, the cream can help calm your airways and reduce the urge to cough. It can also help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to expel. This can be especially helpful for people with respiratory conditions like bronchitis or asthma. By applying the cream to your chest or throat, you can get quick relief from coughing fits and start to feel better. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams that help with coughing fits, start by gathering dried rosemary leaves and carrier oils like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1/4 cup of dried rosemary leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the rosemary to infuse its properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1-2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil to the infused oil and place the jar in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has reached a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the jar from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean container. This rosemary cream can be applied topically to the chest or throat to help soothe and calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are not actually found. They are found in Zingiber officinale (Ginger) and are good for coughing fits because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, allowing for easier breathing.

Shogaols, also known as rosemary, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cough.

Gingerol is not present, as Rosmarinus officinalis is commonly known as Rosemary, not Ginger. However, if you're referring to Gingerol in Zingiber officinale (Ginger), it is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help reduce congestion and soothe the throat, making it easier to cough up mucus and recover from a cough.

Shogaol is not actually found, but rather in Zingiber officinale or Ginger, is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and allowing for easier breathing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a natural remedy for coughing fits. It contains a chemical called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have a cough, it can irritate your throat and make it sore. Gingerol in ginger cream helps to reduce the swelling and soothe the irritation in your throat. This makes it easier to breathe and swallow, and can help to calm a cough. Ginger is also known to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it a good remedy for coughs caused by colds and other infections. When applied topically, ginger cream can provide quick relief from a cough, especially if it's accompanied by a sore throat.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams that help with coughing fits, you will need dried rosemary leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other basic ingredients. First, infuse the dried rosemary leaves in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting them sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process allows the rosemary's healing properties to mix with the oil. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the rosemary leaves. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly add the infused rosemary oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened to a creamy consistency, then pour it into a clean glass jar or tin. Let the cream cool and harden completely before using it to help soothe and calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool (Rosemary) is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the airways, reducing irritation and discomfort.

Linalyl acetate is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help reduce inflammation and calm the muscles in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Romasinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relax.

Caryophyllene is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and relax muscles, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.
Apigenin, also known as rosemary, is good for coughing fits because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream, also known as lavender oil, is good for coughing fits because it has calming and soothing properties. When you have a cough, it can be hard to breathe and feel uncomfortable. Lavender oil helps to calm your body and mind, making it easier to relax and feel better. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can help to reduce swelling and ease the pain in your throat and chest. When you apply the cream to your skin, the lavender oil is absorbed into your body and starts to work its magic. It can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can make coughing fits worse. By calming your body and mind, lavender oil can help to reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits, making it a popular natural remedy for people who suffer from chronic coughing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for coughing fits, you will need a few basic ingredients and some simple equipment. First, gather a handful of fresh rosemary leaves or a teaspoon of dried rosemary, and place it in a small saucepan. Add a cup of oil, such as olive or coconut oil, and heat it on low heat for about 10 minutes, allowing the rosemary to infuse into the oil. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool, then strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Once the oil has cooled completely, mix it with a natural emollient like shea butter or coconut oil, and add a few drops of beeswax to thicken the mixture. Stir the mixture well until it's smooth and creamy, then transfer it into a small tin or jar. This herbal cream can be applied topically to the chest and throat to help soothe and calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Berberine (also known as Rosemary) is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.
Tannins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by coughing.

Alkaloids, specifically rosmarinic acid, are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating the discomfort associated with coughing fits.

Saponins are good for coughing fits because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated airways and reduce coughing spasms, allowing for easier breathing and relief from discomfort.

Hydrastine, also known as rosemary, is good for coughing fits because it has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis cream, also known as goldenseal, has been used for many years to help soothe a cough. The active ingredient in goldenseal, berberine, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the throat and reduce swelling. This can provide relief from a persistent cough and make it easier to breathe. When you have a cough, the tissues in your throat can become inflamed and irritated, leading to discomfort and pain. The berberine in goldenseal cream can help to reduce this inflammation and soothe the affected area, making it a popular remedy for coughs and colds. By using goldenseal cream, you can help to calm your cough and get back to feeling better. The cream can also help to protect your throat from further irritation, which can lead to a faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to help with coughing fits, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you'll need to dry and prepare the rosemary leaves, which can be done by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, mix the rosemary powder with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of about 1 part rosemary to 3 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan with a heat-proof bowl on top, stirring occasionally, until the oil has infused with the rosemary flavor and aroma. After straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any remaining plant particles, you can mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth, spreadable cream. Finally, add a few drops of a soothing essential oil like eucalyptus or peppermint to enhance the cream's therapeutic properties, and store it in a cool, dark place to use as needed to help calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Hydrastis canadensis herbal creams for coughing fits

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for coughing fits because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing spasms.
Carnosic acid is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
Quercetin (Rosemary) is good for coughing fits because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.
Camphor is good for coughing fits because it helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis cream, also known as rosemary cream, has been traditionally used to help with coughing fits. The main reason it's good for this issue is its anti-inflammatory properties. Rosemary contains compounds like camphor and borneol, which help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier. When you have a cough, your airways can become inflamed, making it harder to breathe. The rosemary cream works by soothing the inflamed areas and opening up your airways, allowing you to breathe more easily. The antispasmodic properties of rosemary also help relax the muscles in your throat, making it less likely to go into a coughing fit. This can help give you some relief from a persistent cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalus (Rosemary) herbal cream that helps with coughing fits, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried rosemary leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. First, gently heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches a temperature of about 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Next, add the beeswax and shea butter to the coconut oil and continue heating until both are fully melted. Now, add the dried rosemary leaves to the mixture and let it steep for about 30 minutes to 1 hour to allow the rosemary's properties to infuse into the cream. After the infusion, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the rosemary leaves. Finally, stir in the vitamin E oil and pour the mixture into small containers to cool and solidify. Once the cream is set, it's ready to use - apply it topically to the chest and throat area to help soothe and calm coughing fits.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for coughing fits

intro_desc: Herbal creams can help alleviate coughing fits by providing quick and effective relief. The active ingredients in these creams, such as Eucalyptus globulus, contain oils that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. Eucalyptus globulus oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the throat and respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort.  Another herb used in cough relief creams is Echinacea purpurea. This herb has natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of a cough. Echinacea purpurea also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation in the airways.  Fixing the issue of persistent coughing fits is crucial because it can significantly impact daily life. Coughing fits can disrupt sleep, make it difficult to concentrate, and even lead to dehydration if not managed properly. By using herbal creams containing Eucalyptus globulus and Echinacea purpurea, individuals can experience quick relief and start to feel better. This can improve overall well-being, increase energy levels, and enhance the quality of life. By addressing the underlying cause of the cough and providing the body with the necessary tools to heal, individuals can take control of their health and start to feel more confident and in control.
supplementary_best: For a cough remedy, a combination of herbal creams is more effective than a single herb. The best combination includes Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Zingiber officinale. Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is an antimicrobial herb that helps to reduce the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause coughs. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the throat and reduce irritation.  This combination works well because it targets multiple aspects of coughing: congestion, infection, and inflammation. Eucalyptus globulus addresses congestion, Thymus vulgaris addresses infection, and Zingiber officinale addresses inflammation. This combination is also gentle on the skin and can be applied directly to the chest and throat to provide quick relief from coughing fits.
supplementary_avoid: If you have coughing fits, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these is Asarum europaeum (European wild ginger) cream, which is not safe for use in respiratory problems. Its roots can cause heart problems and interact with other medications, making it a risk for people with coughs. Another cream to avoid is Capsicum annuum (cayenne pepper) cream, which contains a chemical that can irritate your airways, making your cough worse. Also, avoid Paeonia lactiflora (Chinese peony) cream, which can slow down your breathing and worsen respiratory issues. Additionally, avoid Silybum marianum (milk thistle) cream, which can cause allergic reactions and interact with other medications. These herbal creams can cause more harm than good, so it's best to avoid them when you have a cough.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help soothe a cough. It has a thick, sticky texture that coats the throat and reduces inflammation. When we cough, it can irritate the throat and make it feel raw. Honey's thick texture protects the throat and makes it feel better. It's also a natural antiseptic, which means it can help prevent infections from developing.  Ginger is another natural remedy that can help with coughing fits. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the throat and make it feel less irritated. Ginger can be made into a tea by steeping it in hot water, which makes it easy to drink and helps it spread through the body quickly. As it's absorbed, it can help reduce the urge to cough.  Thyme is a herb that's often used to make tea, and it's a natural cough suppressant. It contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the throat. When we drink thyme tea, it can help reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits. This is because it helps to calm down the nerves and muscles in the throat that are responsible for coughing.  Slippery elm is a natural remedy that's often used to make teas and lozenges. It contains mucilage, a thick, gooey substance that can help soothe and protect the throat. When we eat or drink slippery elm, it can help reduce inflammation and make the throat feel less irritated. This can help reduce the urge to cough and make it feel more comfortable.  Slippery elm and honey can also be combined to make a natural cough syrup. The mucilage in slippery elm can help soothe the throat, while the honey can help protect it and reduce inflammation. This can be a powerful combination that can help reduce coughing fits and make the throat feel more comfortable.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience coughing fits often have underlying issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is bronchitis, an inflammation of the airways that can cause a persistent cough. The herb thyme is often used to treat bronchitis because of its antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Another issue is asthma, a condition that causes the airways to constrict and make breathing difficult. The herb eucalyptus is commonly used to relieve asthma symptoms, as its decongestant properties help to open up the airways and make breathing easier. Coughing fits can also be a symptom of tuberculosis, a bacterial infection that affects the lungs. The herb berberine, found in goldenseal, has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help to combat tuberculosis. Additionally, people with coughing fits may experience sinusitis, an inflammation of the sinuses that can cause a persistent cough. The herb echinacea is often used to treat sinusitis, as its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. Finally, coughing fits can be a symptom of allergies, which can cause the airways to constrict and trigger a cough. The herb nettle is commonly used to relieve allergy symptoms, as its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and fight off infections.
